_metadata.description: 100 curated generic task-agnostic text descriptions for DMC distracting environments. Each description has two parts: TASK RELEVANT (focus on agent body/joints/limbs, ignore agent color) and TASK IRRELEVANT (why background and floor should be ignored). Descriptions are shared across all DMC tasks — not task-specific. Reduced from 1000 to 100 diverse, non-redundant descriptions to enable caching of text encoder outputs.
_metadata.structure: TASK RELEVANT: <agent body focus>  TASK IRRELEVANT: <background> Also, <floor>

descriptions: TASK RELEVANT: The shape and pose of the agent's body encode the full control state. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background motion from crowds or environments is a pure visual distractor. Also, Whether the ground looks synthetic or natural is irrelevant to control.

TASK RELEVANT: Joint angles and their temporal evolution are the most informative visual features. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background color, brightness, or texture carries zero task signal. Also, Floor color or material has no effect on the agent's dynamics.

TASK RELEVANT: The vertical alignment and balance of the agent's body are key signals. TASK IRRELEVANT: Rich visual structure in the background — people, flags, scenery — should be ignored. Also, Moving shadows or reflections on the ground are uninformative.

TASK RELEVANT: Agent body color is a cosmetic rendering choice; focus on posture and structure. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background visual complexity does not help predict agent behavior. Also, Bright or dark patches on the floor carry no control information.

TASK RELEVANT: Track the relative positions and angles of the agent's limbs over time. TASK IRRELEVANT: The background is a pure distractor region — suppress it entirely. Also, Floor markings, lines, or patterns provide no task-relevant signal.

TASK RELEVANT: The agent's joint velocities and accelerations encode its dynamic state. TASK IRRELEVANT: Stadium lighting, crowd colors, or urban scenery in the background are noise. Also, The rendered material of the floor — wood, tile, concrete — is irrelevant.

TASK RELEVANT: Focus on the agent's body tree structure and how it deforms during motion. TASK IRRELEVANT: Video-based background content, however realistic, is a distractor to filter out. Also, Ground surface texture variations across episodes are uninformative.

TASK RELEVANT: The agent's center of mass position relative to its support base is critical. TASK IRRELEVANT: Swaying or waving background elements carry no task signal. Also, Sports court markings or lane lines on the floor are irrelevant.

TASK RELEVANT: Observe foot placement and limb-ground contact patterns — these encode the agent's state. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background flickering, temporal noise, or intensity shifts should be ignored. Also, Shadow patterns cast on the floor carry no task-relevant signal.

TASK RELEVANT: The kinematic chain of the agent's body segments is the relevant visual signal. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background scenes from outdoor or indoor environments provide zero task information. Also, High-frequency floor texture patterns are visual noise to suppress.

TASK RELEVANT: Track how each joint's configuration relates to the agent's overall pose. TASK IRRELEVANT: Checkerboard, striped, or random-texture backgrounds are equally task-irrelevant. Also, The floor may look different in every episode — always ignore it.

TASK RELEVANT: The agent's support polygon and grounding pattern carry control-relevant information. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background temporal dynamics — any flickering or moving content — are pure distractors. Also, Glossy or rough floor surface appearance carries no task signal.

TASK RELEVANT: Body-structure features generalize across color and material variations; extract them. TASK IRRELEVANT: The background may differ completely between episodes — ignore it regardless. Also, Real-world floor textures are as irrelevant as synthetic ones.

TASK RELEVANT: The morphological structure of the agent's limbs is the stable, informative feature. TASK IRRELEVANT: Whether the background is brightly or dimly lit carries no control signal. Also, Floor illumination changes do not affect agent dynamics.

TASK RELEVANT: Track whether limbs are grounded or airborne — this describes the locomotion phase. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background contrast changes, edges, or spatial patterns have no task correlation. Also, Gymnasium or arena floor surfaces are irrelevant background content.

TASK RELEVANT: The tilt and orientation of the agent's torso carry important balance information. TASK IRRELEVANT: Crowd motion and sports events visible behind the agent are irrelevant distractors. Also, Lines or markings painted on the floor carry no control information.

TASK RELEVANT: Focus on the velocity and direction of body segments, not their surface rendering. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background brightness variations are unrelated to agent kinematics or task progress. Also, Ground surface color is uninformative for any control task.

TASK RELEVANT: The agent's skeletal configuration is the relevant visual signal; ignore its appearance. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background pixels carry no signal about what action the agent should take. Also, Floor reflections of moving objects are purely cosmetic distractors.

TASK RELEVANT: Represent the agent's body configuration in a way that is invariant to rendering style. TASK IRRELEVANT: Urban scenery, foliage, or sky visible in the background are equally task-irrelevant. Also, The floor's illumination pattern is a rendering artifact to ignore.

TASK RELEVANT: The agent's contact points with the support surface and their forces are informative. TASK IRRELEVANT: Random noise or complex patterns in the background pixels do not encode agent state. Also, Natural ground cover — grass, dirt, pavement — is irrelevant.

TASK RELEVANT: Attend to the topological arrangement of body segments, independent of visual style. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background scene type — indoor, outdoor, or synthetic — does not affect the task. Also, Attending to the floor region only wastes representation capacity.

TASK RELEVANT: The relative movement of limbs and their coordination describe the motor state. TASK IRRELEVANT: High-contrast background edges or contours carry no agent state information. Also, Court lines or ground markings provide zero control information.

TASK RELEVANT: Do not attend to specular highlights or shading on the agent's body surface. TASK IRRELEVANT: A background showing a live sports event provides no task information at all. Also, The playing surface beneath the agent carries no task-relevant signal.

TASK RELEVANT: Observe how body weight shifts across segments during locomotion or balance. TASK IRRELEVANT: Episode-to-episode background changes are irrelevant rendering distractors. Also, Athletic facility flooring patterns are irrelevant visual noise.

TASK RELEVANT: The degrees of freedom at each joint and their current state are the core features. TASK IRRELEVANT: Boundary patterns, gradients, or color shifts in the backdrop are not task-informative. Also, Whether the floor is indoors or outdoors does not matter for the task.

TASK RELEVANT: Track joint flexion and extension patterns — they directly describe the control state. TASK IRRELEVANT: Frame-to-frame background changes are visual noise with no task signal. Also, The ground surface should be treated as a distractor region to suppress.

TASK RELEVANT: The agent's appearance details — color, texture, shininess — are irrelevant rendering choices. TASK IRRELEVANT: Dynamic video textures in the background carry no control signal whatsoever. Also, Floor color variations across episodes do not affect agent physics.

TASK RELEVANT: Extract features from the agent's geometry that are stable across lighting conditions. TASK IRRELEVANT: Illumination shifts or flicker in the background are not task-informative. Also, Compression artifacts or noise on the floor are irrelevant to control.

TASK RELEVANT: The spatial extent and orientation of the agent's limbs are the key visual features. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background regions contain only distractor information — suppress them. Also, The visual quality of the ground surface is uninformative for control.

TASK RELEVANT: How the agent's body deforms and moves over time is the control-relevant signal. TASK IRRELEVANT: Whatever real-world content appears behind the agent should be ignored. Also, Floor grid or tile boundaries carry no task-relevant information.

TASK RELEVANT: The angular rates of the agent's joints are informative temporal features. TASK IRRELEVANT: Whether the background shows a crowd, a landscape, or random noise is equally irrelevant. Also, Random texture variations on the floor are irrelevant distractors.

TASK RELEVANT: Focus on the agent's limb-ground interaction — grounding, sliding, lifting — as key signals. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background hue changes are rendering choices unrelated to the agent's dynamics. Also, Grainy or noisy floor appearance does not affect agent control.

TASK RELEVANT: The agent's body outline and silhouette change encodes its kinematic state. TASK IRRELEVANT: Moving objects or people in the background provide zero control-relevant information. Also, Dynamic floor reflections are purely cosmetic and should be suppressed.

TASK RELEVANT: Build features that are invariant to the agent's rendered color and material. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background footage — whether from a sports event or a nature video — is always irrelevant. Also, Lane markers or grid lines on the floor are visual noise.

TASK RELEVANT: The agent's stance width and the distribution of its weight carry task information. TASK IRRELEVANT: The background should always be treated as uninformative noise, regardless of content. Also, Floor surface finish — shiny, matte, rough — has no correlation with agent dynamics.

TASK RELEVANT: Observe how the agent's segments accelerate and decelerate during a movement. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background noise — spatial or temporal — has no bearing on what action to take. Also, Whether the ground appears dark or light carries no task signal.

TASK RELEVANT: The agent's physical configuration is the stable feature; its appearance is not. TASK IRRELEVANT: Patterned backdrops are decorative rendering choices and should be suppressed. Also, Shadows on the floor moving over time are uninformative distractors.

TASK RELEVANT: Attend to the articulation of joints and the angles between connected limb segments. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background brightness, saturation, or contrast carry no task-informative signal. Also, The floor may show tiles, wood, or carpet — all equally irrelevant.

TASK RELEVANT: Joint positions and their spatial relationships encode the complete agent state. TASK IRRELEVANT: Busy or noisy background patterns do not encode any information about agent state. Also, Lighting patterns projected on the floor are irrelevant rendering effects.

TASK RELEVANT: Focus on the agent's body relative to its ground contact points and support polygon. TASK IRRELEVANT: Objects or scenes drifting in the background provide no task-relevant information. Also, The floor color has no effect on the agent's contact dynamics.

TASK RELEVANT: The agent's physical form and its deformation over time carry the control signal. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background scene dynamics — crowd, flags, video — have no correlation with the task. Also, Whether the floor looks shiny or matte is irrelevant to control.

TASK RELEVANT: Ignore the glossiness or matte finish of the agent's body; focus on its configuration. TASK IRRELEVANT: Temporal flicker or spatial patterns in the background region should be ignored. Also, Visual ground texture patterns are cosmetic and task-irrelevant.

TASK RELEVANT: The kinematics of the agent — positions, velocities, accelerations — are the visual signal. TASK IRRELEVANT: Attending to the background would only degrade task performance. Also, Floor texture complexity has no relationship to the task.

TASK RELEVANT: Track the agent's grounding state and how it shifts during a movement sequence. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background video content, regardless of how realistic it looks, is always irrelevant. Also, Ground surfaces from outdoor scenes are equally irrelevant.

TASK RELEVANT: The agent's lighting-induced appearance changes are not informative for control. TASK IRRELEVANT: High-contrast patterns or edges behind the agent are decorative visual noise. Also, The floor region of the scene carries no task-relevant information.

TASK RELEVANT: Observe how the limbs coordinate during walking, running, or balancing. TASK IRRELEVANT: The background provides zero bits of task-useful information in any episode. Also, Gymnasium or arena floors are irrelevant background content.

TASK RELEVANT: The spatial relationships within the agent's kinematic chain are the key features. TASK IRRELEVANT: Whether the background contains people, objects, or abstract patterns is irrelevant. Also, Floor illumination or lighting variation is uninformative.

TASK RELEVANT: Focus on the agent's body structure and how it relates to the support surface. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background contrast changes have no effect on the agent's dynamics or rewards. Also, All floor visual features are irrelevant to the control task.

TASK RELEVANT: The relationship between body orientation and the direction of motion is informative. TASK IRRELEVANT: Crowd or audience motion behind the agent is a distractor to be suppressed. Also, Ground color has no bearing on the forces acting on the agent.

TASK RELEVANT: The agent's skeletal pose — not its color or texture — encodes the control state. TASK IRRELEVANT: Strong background contours do not carry any agent state information. Also, Floor surface appearance differences across tasks are irrelevant.

TASK RELEVANT: Attend to the coordination between upper and lower body segments during motion. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background regions should be treated as random noise regardless of their content. Also, The shadow of the agent on the floor is not informative.

TASK RELEVANT: The agent's postural stability and deviations from upright encode balance information. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background scene content changes between episodes — always ignore it. Also, Changes in floor brightness due to lighting carry no signal.

TASK RELEVANT: Track how each limb's endpoint position changes over consecutive timesteps. TASK IRRELEVANT: Whether the backdrop is a sports arena, nature scene, or white wall is irrelevant. Also, Reflections on the floor surface are irrelevant visual content.

TASK RELEVANT: The agent's body configuration encodes what it is doing, not how it looks. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background movement — including any flickering or temporal variation — is noise. Also, Floor markings — random or structured — carry no control information.

TASK RELEVANT: Focus on body segments and their relative orientations in the image plane. TASK IRRELEVANT: Any visual content behind the agent is a distractor unrelated to the control task. Also, The floor provides zero bits of information about what action to take.

TASK RELEVANT: The agent's swing and stance phases encode its locomotion dynamics directly. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background imagery of any kind should be treated as visual noise to suppress. Also, Natural ground textures — grass, dirt, gravel — are irrelevant.

TASK RELEVANT: Observe the agent's body velocity — fast or slow, toward or away — as a key feature. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background light variations — bright patches, dark regions — carry no task signal. Also, Whether the ground appears synthetic or natural is irrelevant.

TASK RELEVANT: The agent's postural lean and center of mass offset are control-informative features. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background scene dynamics have no correlation with the agent's state transitions. Also, Floor pixel values carry no task-relevant information.

TASK RELEVANT: Track the rotation of each body segment across consecutive frames. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background texture complexity has no relationship to the agent's task. Also, Lines, markings, or patterns painted on the floor are irrelevant.

TASK RELEVANT: The agent's body geometry is the invariant feature; its surface properties are not. TASK IRRELEVANT: Colorful, moving, or visually complex backgrounds are equally irrelevant. Also, The visual material of the floor — wood, tile, concrete — is irrelevant.

TASK RELEVANT: Focus on how the agent's limbs interact with the ground — contact, push-off, swing. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background color shifts between episodes are irrelevant rendering distractors. Also, Whether the floor is gray, green, or blue is irrelevant.

TASK RELEVANT: The agent's angular momentum and rotation rate carry dynamics information. TASK IRRELEVANT: Noisy or unstable background imagery is not task-informative in any way. Also, The ground surface should be treated as a distractor to suppress.

TASK RELEVANT: Observe the agent's body alignment relative to the vertical axis at each timestep. TASK IRRELEVANT: Objects or crowd members moving in the background have no effect on the task. Also, Attending to the floor region hurts the representation quality.

TASK RELEVANT: The connectivity and articulation structure of the agent's body is the stable feature. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background regions contain only task-irrelevant visual information — always. Also, Floor surface textures carry no information about the agent's state.

TASK RELEVANT: Focus on the agent's geometric configuration, which is invariant to visual rendering. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background footage from a sports event, nature video, or synthetic scene is always irrelevant. Also, Visual patterns on the ground surface can be ignored entirely.

TASK RELEVANT: Track the agent's torso orientation and how it relates to its limb positions. TASK IRRELEVANT: Dynamic background content has no correlation with agent dynamics. Also, Ground surface visual variation across frames is uninformative.

TASK RELEVANT: The agent's body motion trajectory encodes its control state across time. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background brightness, color, or motion provides no useful task information. Also, The floor's color is an irrelevant rendering choice to suppress.

TASK RELEVANT: Attend to the agent's body outline and how it evolves — this is the kinematic state. TASK IRRELEVANT: Shadows, reflections, or glare visible in the background carry no task signal. Also, Court lines or ground markings provide no control information.

TASK RELEVANT: The relative velocities of body segments encode the agent's motor command history. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background scenes — however photorealistic — are always task-irrelevant distractors. Also, High-frequency floor texture patterns are irrelevant noise.

TASK RELEVANT: Observe the agent's body posture from a structural perspective, not a visual one. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background regions should be suppressed regardless of their visual complexity. Also, The floor may look different every episode — always ignore it.

TASK RELEVANT: The agent's joint-angle trajectory over time describes its movement pattern. TASK IRRELEVANT: Any distractor content in the background region carries no control signal. Also, Gymnasium or arena floor markings are irrelevant to the task.

TASK RELEVANT: The instantaneous pose of the agent's body encodes its current control state. TASK IRRELEVANT: Whether the background shows crowds, buildings, or nature is equally irrelevant. Also, The shadow of the agent cast on the floor is not informative.

TASK RELEVANT: Ignore the agent's surface color and texture; focus on its structural configuration. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background footage with any amount of motion should be entirely filtered out. Also, Floor reflections or shadows have no bearing on the agent's dynamics.

TASK RELEVANT: The agent's body segments and their relative positions form the task-relevant state. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background patterns, whether static or dynamic, provide no task information. Also, Floor markings may look complex but none carry control information.

TASK RELEVANT: Focus on the agent's kinematic state — positions, angles — not its visual appearance. TASK IRRELEVANT: The background color or brightness at any given episode is irrelevant. Also, Ground surface material — grass, concrete, carpet — is irrelevant.

TASK RELEVANT: The agent's body silhouette and pose change together to describe its state. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background texture patterns — stripes, grids, checkerboards — are all irrelevant. Also, Bright or dark floor patches are uninformative to the task.

TASK RELEVANT: Track the spatial layout of the agent's body and how it shifts over time. TASK IRRELEVANT: Any background content — static or dynamic — is a distractor region to ignore. Also, Real-world floor textures are as irrelevant as synthetic ones.

TASK RELEVANT: The agent's physical degrees of freedom and their instantaneous values are the signal. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background distractors vary between episodes — treat them all as irrelevant noise. Also, Floor color changes have zero correlation with agent kinematics.

TASK RELEVANT: Observe the agent's limb endpoints and their distances from each other and the ground. TASK IRRELEVANT: Whatever is behind the agent — crowd, sky, abstract pattern — is irrelevant. Also, The floor region of the scene should always be suppressed.

TASK RELEVANT: The agent's body geometry at each frame encodes a complete description of its state. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background visual content carries no information about the control task. Also, Lighting effects on the ground surface are not task-informative.

TASK RELEVANT: Focus on the structural relationships between body parts — not their visual properties. TASK IRRELEVANT: Video or photographic backgrounds are always irrelevant to the control task. Also, Floor texture features distract from the agent's actual state representation.

TASK RELEVANT: Track the agent's overall body posture and its deviation from a reference configuration. TASK IRRELEVANT: Stadium crowds, arena lighting, or broadcast graphics are pure visual distractors. Also, Grainy or noisy floor appearance does not affect agent control.

TASK RELEVANT: The agent's muscular or joint forces are expressed through its body configuration. TASK IRRELEVANT: Backgrounds from sports arenas or outdoor environments provide no task information. Also, Any decorative markings on the ground surface can be ignored.

TASK RELEVANT: Observe the entire agent body as a connected kinematic structure in each frame. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background content — whatever its source — is always task-irrelevant. Also, Changes in floor illumination do not affect agent dynamics.

TASK RELEVANT: The phase and timing of the agent's gait cycle are the control-relevant features. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background patterns and textures carry no signal about agent behavior. Also, Ground markings — whether random or structured — are irrelevant.

TASK RELEVANT: Extract body-pose features that remain consistent regardless of rendering environment. TASK IRRELEVANT: The background is always a distractor — ignore it regardless of how realistic it looks. Also, Floor surface properties carry no information about agent state.

TASK RELEVANT: The agent's postural dynamics — how its pose changes over time — encode its motor state. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background footage from any domain should be treated as uninformative noise. Also, The floor material and appearance are irrelevant to the control task.

TASK RELEVANT: Track the agent's body and how its configuration changes with each timestep. TASK IRRELEVANT: Whether the background is brightly lit or dark, moving or static, is equally irrelevant. Also, The playing surface beneath the agent carries no task signal.

TASK RELEVANT: The agent's body segments form a rigid-body tree — track its configuration, not its color. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background content provides zero task-useful information regardless of its nature. Also, Sports court surfaces and their markings are visual distractors.

TASK RELEVANT: Attend to how body parts coordinate during the task — this coordination is the signal. TASK IRRELEVANT: Temporal variations in the background carry no task information whatsoever. Also, Any floor visual feature is irrelevant to the control task.

TASK RELEVANT: The agent's body shape changes reveal its internal control state and dynamics. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background imagery is always decorative and should be suppressed by the encoder. Also, Whether the ground appears dark or light carries no task signal.

TASK RELEVANT: Focus on the agent's joint angles and velocities — the complete kinematic state. TASK IRRELEVANT: No background feature can help predict the agent's state or optimal action. Also, Floor color and texture carry no information about agent dynamics.

TASK RELEVANT: The agent's body pose, velocity, and contact state together describe the control state. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background distractors change between episodes but are always irrelevant. Also, Visual noise on the floor surface should be filtered out entirely.

TASK RELEVANT: Represent the agent's body in a way that captures structure, not surface appearance. TASK IRRELEVANT: Whatever background content appears in an episode is always a visual distractor. Also, The ground surface beneath the agent carries no task-relevant information.

TASK RELEVANT: The agent's body motion — how it accelerates and decelerates — encodes its dynamics. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background regions provide no signal about the agent's state or the optimal action. Also, Floor texture complexity has no relationship to the control task.

TASK RELEVANT: Track the relative positions and velocities of all agent body segments together. TASK IRRELEVANT: Any video-based or photographic background should be suppressed as a distractor. Also, The visual pattern on the ground is a distractor to suppress.

TASK RELEVANT: The agent's body configuration is the task-relevant feature; its color is irrelevant. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background scenes change between runs but carry no task-relevant information. Also, Ground surfaces from outdoor and indoor scenes are equally irrelevant.

TASK RELEVANT: Observe the agent's body structure and motion dynamics as the primary task signal. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background pixels always provide zero bits of task-useful information. Also, The floor is a visual distractor that should be suppressed in the representation.

TASK RELEVANT: The agent's limb positions encode what phase of motion it is in — attend to them. TASK IRRELEVANT: Background regions are always irrelevant, whether moving or static, bright or dark. Also, Floor surface properties are cosmetic and carry no task signal.

TASK RELEVANT: A color-invariant body-pose representation captures the control state reliably. TASK IRRELEVANT: The background is a distractor that changes across episodes and carries no task signal. Also, Floor visual variation across frames does not affect agent dynamics.
